zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Hugh Massey於1380年出生在英國英格蘭爾可努恰福德時，他的父親Lord John Massey36歲，母親Alice Worsley32歲。他於1399年與Lady Matilda de Timperley在英國英格蘭藍卡爾利斯頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1420年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾利斯頓享年40歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Hugh Massey于1380年出生在英国英格兰爾可努恰福德时，他的父亲Lord John Massey36岁，母亲Alice Worsley32岁。他于1399年与Lady Matilda de Timperley在英国英格兰藍卡爾利斯頓结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1420年在英国英格兰藍卡爾利斯頓享年40岁。

nameMeanings.name: Massey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Massy, a pet form of Matthew . Compare Mace .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from one or more of several places in Normandy, such as Macey (Manche), Massy (Seine-Maritime), Macé-sur-Orne (Orne), and La Ferté Macé (Orne).

variants.name: Mace
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Masse, probably a shortened form of Matthew (compare Massey and Massett ) or possibly of Thomas .
French and Breton (Macé): from the French personal name Macé, a vernacular form of Mathieu ‘Matthew’. Compare Masse 5 and Maze 3.

French (Macé): habitational name from Macé or La Ferté-Macé in Orne, the former so named from Gallo-Roman Macciacum (from the personal name Maccius + the locative suffix -acum), the latter from the personal name Macé (see 2 above).

variants.name: Massee
meanings: Possibly an altered form of English   Massey  .
French (Massée): from the feminine form of Massé (see Masse ). This surname is rare in France.

variants.name: Massa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Antonio, Silvio, Annamarie, Biagio, Caesar, Carmela, Dante, Elisabetta, Ennio.

Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Massa, for example Massa Lubrense or Massa di Somma, both in Naples province, or Masse d'Albe in Aquila, all named from medieval Latin massa ‘farm, estate’ (from Latin massa ‘mass, lump, pile’, Greek maza). This surname is also found in southern France (see Massey 5).

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English personal name Ma(t)thew, a vernacular form of the Greek New Testament name Matthias, Matthaios, which is ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Matityahu ‘gift of God’. This was taken into Latin as Mat(t)hias and Matthaeus respectively, the former being used for the twelfth apostle (who replaced Judas Iscariot) and the latter for the author of the first Gospel. In many European languages this distinction is reflected in different name forms. The commonest vernacular forms of the personal name, including English Matthew, Old French Matheu, Spanish Mateo, Italian Matteo, Portuguese Mateus, Catalan and Occitan Mateu are generally derived from the form Matthaeus. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, including German Matthias , Greek Mathias and Mattheos, Assyrian/Chaldean Matti , Czech Matěj, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Matej . The name Matthew is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Mathai ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. Compare Mathew , see also Matthews .

variants.name: Macey
meanings: Americanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Macej or Mačej and Polish Maciej , derivatives of the Latin personal name Matthaeus (see Matthew ).

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Hugh Massey was born in 1380, in Knutsford, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Lord John Massey, was 36 and his mother, Alice Worsley, was 32. He married Lady Matilda de Timperley in 1399, in Rixton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1420, in Rixton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Hugh Massey est né en 1380, à Knutsford, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Lord John Massey, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Alice Worsley, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Lady Matilda de Timperley en 1399, à Rixton, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1420, à Rixton, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 40 ans.

Er hat 1399 in Rixton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Lady Matilda de Timperley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1420 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Rixton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Hugh Massey è nato il nel 1380 nel Knutsford, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Lord John Massey, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Alice Worsley, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Lady Matilda de Timperley nel 1399, nel Rixton, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1420, nel Rixton, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 40 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Massey(이)가 1380년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 누츠퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord John Massey(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Worsley(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1399년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 리스턴에서 Lady Matilda de Timperley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1420년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 리스턴에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.
